CRM.rationale: Salesforce shows solid revenue growth and improving margins, but a P/E of 71.7x prices in much of the upside, leaving limited risk-adjusted reward over a one-year horizon.
key_factors: P/E diluted at 71.71x, near the low end of its 1y range (70.1–1063.7) but still elevated
Revenue grew to $34.86B FY2024 from prior year, with quarterly run-rate ~$9.29B
Net margin of 11.9% and operating margin of 14.4% show profitability but ROE is low at 6.9%
Stock up 53.2% over the past year, momentum strong but valuation stretched
Recent news includes a downgrade to Hold citing overvaluation (2024-03-23)

DIS.rationale: Strong earnings inflection and positive momentum are partially offset by a still-rich 53.7x PE on thin 4.7% margins after a 35% three-month rally, leaving modest but real upside.

Stock already up +35.4% in 3 months and hitting 52-week highs, much of the recovery may be priced in
PE diluted at 53.7x with net margin only 4.7% and ROE 4% — valuation remains rich despite improving fundamentals
Positive news flow: analyst 15% upside target, bullish streaming narrative, but board governance concerns around Iger

KO.rationale: KO offers defensive quality at a fair-but-not-cheap valuation (PE 24.8x) with strong margins and ROE, but Q4 EPS weakness and lack of growth catalysts cap the risk-adjusted reward at a mild long lean.

CSCO.rationale: Declining revenue trajectory is the dominant risk, but PE at the bottom of its range, strong margins, a clean balance sheet, and the just-completed Splunk acquisition offer enough offsetting reward for a mild long lean.

INTC.rationale: Extreme valuation (110x PE) against barely-breakeven fundamentals (0.2% operating margin, 1.5% ROE) makes the risk-reward unattractive — the turnaround story is real but already priced in with no earnings traction to justify it.

PG.rationale: PG trades at the top of its valuation range with declining quarterly revenue and EPS, so the risk of multiple compression at premium multiples outweighs the defensive margin of safety over a one-year horizon.

DOW.rationale: Deteriorating fundamentals — declining revenue, near-zero margins, and a PE at 70x — outweigh the low price-to-sales multiple, making the risk-reward unattractive over a one-year horizon absent a clear catalyst for earnings recovery.
